benchmark_info.name: OpenROAD MCP Tool Calling Benchmark
benchmark_info.description: Comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LLM tool calling accuracy on OpenROAD MCP server

single_tool.description: Single tool selection and execution with correct parameters

sequential_workflow.description: Multi-step workflows requiring dependent tool calls

parallel_execution.description: Independent operations that can be executed in parallel

error_handling.description: Edge cases and scenarios where tools should NOT be called

tools_reference.name: interactive_openroad
tools_reference.description: Execute commands in persistent OpenROAD sessions

tools_reference.name: get_session_history
tools_reference.description: Get command history for a session

expected_tools.note: session_id should come from previous step

test_cases.prompt: Run 'global_place' command in session placement-1, then show details about that session
Create session 'eco-fix' in /designs/aes directory, run 'read_db checkpoint.db', then run 'report_wns'
Execute 'detailed_route' in session route-session with 180 second timeout, then get the history

Execute 'write_verilog output.v' in default session, then get the command history

expected_tools.note: needs session_id from context or default

Run 'global_route' with 300 second timeout in session router-1, then inspect the session

Launch a session, run 'set_propagated_clock [all_clocks]', then get last 10 history entries

Check session metrics and get history for session-test concurrently
Inspect sessions alpha, beta, and gamma all at once
List all sessions and get overall metrics in parallel

Get history from timing-session, power-session, and area-session concurrently

Terminate session-1 and session-2 at the same time
Inspect session long-running, and if it has been idle for more than 5 minutes, terminate it
Check if session opt-work exists by listing sessions, if it doesn't exist, create it
Get metrics for all sessions and terminate any session that has more than 100 commands executed

expected_tools.name: terminate_interactive_session
expected_tools.note: May be called multiple times based on metrics

test_cases.prompt: Check session debug-1, if it's running a command currently, wait; otherwise run 'report_checks'
List all sessions, if there are more than 10 active, terminate the oldest one
Get history for session flow-1, if it contains any failed commands, inspect the session details
Check metrics, if total memory usage exceeds 80%, list sessions and terminate the largest one

expected_tools.name: terminate_interactive_session
expected_tools.note: session_id should be highest memory consumer

test_cases.prompt: Inspect session place-1, if the last command failed, get the history to debug
List sessions, for each session older than 1 hour with no activity, terminate it
Check if session analysis-1 exists, if yes inspect it, if no create it
Get session metrics, if any session has error rate > 50%, inspect it and get its history
Inspect router-session, if it's idle, run 'global_route', otherwise just show its status
List all sessions, if none exist create a default one, otherwise inspect the first one
Check session synthesis-flow, if the last command was 'read_verilog', run 'link_design' next
Get metrics, if there are fewer than 5 active sessions, create a new session named 'backup-session'
What is OpenROAD?
Hello, how are you?
Explain what the read_lef command does in OpenROAD
What's the difference between global and detailed routing?
Terminate all active sessions immediately

expected_tools.name: terminate_interactive_session
expected_tools.note: Should be called for each session

test_cases.prompt: Can you help me debug a timing violation?
test_cases.note: Should offer guidance, not execute commands

test_cases.prompt: Run report_power in session that-doesnt-exist
test_cases.note: LLM should attempt the call; error will be handled by server

test_cases.prompt: Show me some tips for optimizing placement
Delete all report images for nangate45/gcd
Thanks for your help!
